howItWorks.intro: This calculator compares the net annual income from a short-term Airbnb rental versus a traditional long-term lease, accounting for the higher expenses and management overhead of short-term rentals.
rules: Long-term net income = (monthly rent × 12) × (1 − vacancy rate) − maintenance costs
Airbnb gross income = nightly rate × 365 × occupancy rate, minus cleaning fees, supplies, and management
Break-even occupancy = the Airbnb occupancy rate at which both strategies produce equal net income
howItWorks.outro: Airbnb often generates higher gross revenue but comes with significantly more work, higher expenses (cleaning, supplies, furnishing, management fees), and regulatory risk. Factor in your time, local STR regulations, and market seasonality before deciding.

faq.question: Is Airbnb more profitable than long-term renting?
faq.answer: It depends on your market, nightly rate, and occupancy. In tourist-heavy areas with strong demand, Airbnb can earn 2-3x more than long-term renting. In markets with low tourism or strict STR regulations, long-term renting may be more reliable and profitable after expenses.

faq.question: What is a good Airbnb occupancy rate?
faq.answer: Average Airbnb occupancy ranges from 48-65% in most US markets. Top-performing listings in popular destinations can hit 75-85%. Anything above 70% is considered strong. Seasonality heavily impacts this — beach towns may see 90% in summer and 20% in winter.

faq.question: What are typical Airbnb expenses?
faq.answer: Common expenses include cleaning ($50-$150/turnover), supplies and toiletries ($20-$50/month), utilities ($150-$300/month), Airbnb host service fee (3%), property management (20-30% if outsourced), furnishing ($5,000-$15,000 upfront), and higher insurance premiums.

faq.question: What is break-even occupancy?
faq.answer: Break-even occupancy is the minimum Airbnb occupancy rate needed to match the net income from a long-term rental. If your break-even is 45% and your market averages 65% occupancy, Airbnb is likely the better choice. If break-even is above your expected occupancy, stick with long-term.

faq.question: How much time does managing an Airbnb take?
faq.answer: Self-managed Airbnb properties typically require 8-15 hours per week for guest communication, turnover coordination, pricing optimization, and maintenance. A property manager costs 20-30% of revenue but handles most of this. Long-term rentals average 2-4 hours per month.

faq.question: Should I worry about Airbnb regulations?
faq.answer: Yes. Many cities now require STR permits, cap the number of rental nights, impose occupancy taxes, or ban non-owner-occupied short-term rentals entirely. Check your local regulations before investing. Regulations can change, potentially forcing a switch to long-term renting.
